message:
fix(fnp-python): testing_assert_string_equal honors full Python separator set (2axo)

Self-review on commit dc2fcb6 (u98f) found that the hand-rolled
lines_keepends helper only recognised \n / \r / \r\n as line separators.
Python's str.splitlines(keepends=True) — which numpy.testing's
assert_string_equal uses — also splits on \v, \f, \x1c, \x1d, \x1e,
\x85, U+2028, and U+2029. On those separators our diff output diverged
from numpy's, breaking the exact-format parity guarantee.

Fix: route through actual.splitlines(True) / desired.splitlines(True)
via pyo3 so the canonical Python separator set is used. Drop the
lines_keepends helper entirely.

Drive-by: tighten the diff-line filter to drop matched `  ` lines
(numpy only emits `- `/`+ `/`? ` markers), so the assertion message
matches numpy character-for-character even when context lines exist.

Adds a regression probe exercising \v, \f, \x1e separators against
numpy's reference output to lock in the broader coverage.

br-franken_numpy-2axo

Reconcile the docs so the live inventory matches the actual fixture corpus.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:54:10.700125319Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:07.898347018Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:07.898084862Z","close_reason":"Docs reconciled to verified RNG conformance corpus; removed stale 40-distribution claim wording","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-29r9","title":"numpy.fabs python wrapper parity","description":"Add fnp_python.fabs passthrough wrapper around numpy.fabs(x, /, out=None, *, where=True, casting='same_kind', order='K', dtype=None, subok=True). Computes the absolute value element-wise as a float. Include oracle test covering: positive/negative float scalars, 1-D array with mixed signs, integer array promoted to float, NaN preservation, +inf/-inf handling, and 2-D array.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:08:42.561431399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T08:20:46Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T08:20:46Z","close_reason":"Completed br-franken_numpy-29r9: numpy.fabs parity was already present on main","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"franken_numpy-2axo","title":"[BUG] testing_assert_string_equal lines_keepends incomplete separator set","description":"testing_assert_string_equal native port (commit dc2fcb6) added a free
fn lines_keepends in fnp-python/src/lib.rs that mirrors Python's
str.splitlines(keepends=True) for \n, \r, \r\n only. Python actually
recognizes a wider separator set: \v (\x0b), \f (\x0c), \x1c, \x1d,
\x1e, \x85,  ,  .

Self-review found in last-3-commits sweep on 2026-04-25.

For typical test input (newline-terminated lines) the divergence is
invisible, but a string containing e.g. \v will tokenise differently
than numpy's reference and produce a non-matching diff message,
breaking the exact-format parity guarantee documented in the commit.

Fix: drop lines_keepends and route through Python's stdlib via
`actual.into_pyobject(py)?.call_method1("splitlines", (true,))?` so
the canonical separator set is used. Cleaner, smaller, and correct.

Acceptance:
- lines_keepends helper deleted.
- testing_assert_string_equal calls actual.splitlines(True) /
  desired.splitlines(True) via pyo3.
- Existing testing_assert_string_equal parity test passes.
- Optional: extra parity oracle exercising \v and \r-only inputs to
  prove the broader separator set is honored (gated on
  numpy_available).","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-25T19:46:24.338509071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T19:46:46.112521028Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Bind to fnp-random backends, preserve NumPy constructor/error semantics, and add Python oracle tests for random_raw, state, random, integers, normal, permutation, choice, and bytes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T01:10:13.907626023Z","created_by":"cod-franken_numpy","updated_at":"2026-04-24T02:02:11.995733229Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T02:02:11.993922961Z","close_reason":"Exposed numpy.random Generator, bit generators, RandomState, and oracle-tested fnp-python bindings","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"franken_numpy-2h2s.1","depends_on_id":"franken_numpy-2h2s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-24T01:10:13.907626023Z","created_by":"cod-franken_numpy","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
